name: H.I.R.A Growth Log
subtitle: How Herwanto Interface for Responsive Assistance grew from an installable assistant into a more reliable personal operating system.

metrics.label: Days of build acceleration
metrics.note: From first installable PWA commit to governed, self-improving core.

metrics.label: Tracked upgrade commits
metrics.note: Grouped from git history through the growth-log minisite.

metrics.label: Memory buckets
metrics.note: Profile, preferences, teaching, business, sports, topic profiles, corrections, reflections, source notes, and more — now with priority-weighted prompt injection.

metrics.label: Specialist live domains
metrics.note: Weather, MUIS, Gmail, Liverpool/F1, documents, classlists, and web links.

capabilityRings.name: Interface
capabilityRings.description: Installable PWA, mobile cockpit, chat, files, dashboard, notifications, health surfaces.

capabilityRings.name: Security
capabilityRings.description: Telegram fail-closed allowlist, web API token throttling, constant-time auth checks, safer rate-limit keys, /forget confirmation, file size guards, and Redis job locks.

capabilityRings.name: Context
capabilityRings.description: Timetable, Google Calendar, MTL classlists, checked availability slots, Drive references, file memory, topic profiles, conversation summaries, relevant-memory retrieval, and intent lensing.

capabilityRings.name: Live Knowledge
capabilityRings.description: Weather, MUIS, Google News, Tavily search, fetch URL, source notes, live-check hints, structured Liverpool and F1 briefers.

capabilityRings.name: Self-Improvement
capabilityRings.description: Priority-weighted memory prompt, relevant recall, intent lens, proactive workload insights, implicit correction detection, weekly consolidation, and conversation summarisation.

chapters.title: Hira becomes installable
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A moved from a bot-shaped assistant toward a dedicated private app surface.
details: Installable PWA introduced.
Railway start mode added for the PWA.
Agenda, chat formatting, grouped school calendar variables, and task surfaces became part of the app shell.
chapters.impact: Anyone could now understand H.I.R.A as a personal cockpit, not just a Telegram command bot.

chapters.title: The app gets a body
chapters.summary: The PWA became visually and ergonomically distinctive: a cockpit, clock, notification controls, and branded H.I.R.A core.
details: Mobile cockpit and home branding polished.
Notification states, clock, asset refresh, and installed-app behavior were refined.
Chat streaming and backend responsiveness improved.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A started feeling like a real daily-use system rather than a prototype page.

chapters.title: Hira learns to nudge, follow up, and carry files
chapters.summary: The assistant gained more practical work surfaces: attachments, Gmail-informed follow-ups, nudges in chat, and push reminders.
details: Chat attachment uploads added.
Gmail details began powering email follow-ups.
Push reminders bridged into the PWA chat.
Railway memory usage and overnight jobs were hardened.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A became less passive: it could hold pending work and push useful reminders back into the app.

chapters.title: The daily workload dashboard takes shape
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A started showing workload state instead of only answering direct questions.
details: NEA weather arrived.
Marking load dashboards became class/work-type aware.
Daily score, workload trend, voice graph, signal module, and mobile dashboard states were refined.
Weather routing and header alignment improved.
chapters.impact: The home screen became a scan-friendly operational view of Herwanto's day.

chapters.title: Hira becomes more careful with facts
chapters.summary: The assistant gained stronger source discipline for prayer, location, schedule, and school data.
details: MUIS prayer and khutbah behavior became source-grounded.
PWA memory guardrails and notification worker mode improved stability.
Async uploads and notification health tools were added.
MTL Drive, classlist context, score entry, and analysis tools arrived.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A became safer to trust for high-friction daily details: prayers, classlists, files, and score workflows.

chapters.title: Sports and browsing stop being guesswork
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A learned to read links, respect current Liverpool/F1 context, and avoid stale sports corrections.
details: Direct URL fetching added.
F1 and Liverpool preferences became first-class context.
Liverpool quick-route failure was fixed.
Structured Liverpool and F1 brief tools were added.
chapters.impact: When Herwanto asks about current teams, line-ups, results, or rumours, H.I.R.A now knows to verify instead of bluffing.

chapters.title: Hobby-plan architecture hardening
chapters.summary: The backend was tuned for Railway Hobby and made less fragile under real use.
details: Chat/upload concurrency increased responsibly.
Upload jobs moved into a bounded queue.
Web scheduling separated from the worker service.
Redis can be required in production.
Admin status endpoint exposes runtime, queues, models, notifications, and integrations.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A gained a more reliable server shape: clearer ownership, better queues, and visible health.

chapters.title: Hira gets cleaner long-term memory
chapters.summary: Memory was split into clearer buckets, then upgraded with topic profiles for new interests.
details: Memory buckets added for teaching, business, sports, constraints, summaries, and topic profiles.
Model routing became configurable for quick, structured, agentic, and deep work.
Topic profiles now store what to track, preferred angle, live facts, stable context, and cadence.
chapters.impact: New interests can become structured knowledge lenses instead of scattered notes.

chapters.title: Hira gets a public growth story
chapters.summary: A dedicated minisite now charts H.I.R.A's growth from inception to current capability.
details: Growth story added at /growth and /hira-growth.
Timeline, capability rings, current capabilities, and update protocol are driven by JSON data.
Future upgrades can append a chapter to pwa/hira-growth-data.json.
chapters.impact: Anyone can now understand how much H.I.R.A has grown without reading the codebase or commit history.

chapters.title: Hira starts learning from corrections
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a deterministic learning loop for corrections and meaningful chats.
details: Correction ledger added as high-priority memory.
Self-reflection notes now capture important learning moments from PWA and Telegram chats.
The system prompt now treats corrections and reflections as durable behaviour guidance.
chapters.impact: When Herwanto corrects H.I.R.A or introduces an important direction, that lesson can persist and shape future answers.

chapters.title: Browsing starts compounding into memory
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a source-backed intake tool so useful research can become durable context instead of one-off answers.
details: New source_notes memory bucket added.
remember_source_insight stores topic, source, URL, insight, durability, and confidence.
Web, link, news, Liverpool, and F1 prompts now expose the source-note tool.
chapters.impact: When H.I.R.A researches a new topic, it can keep stable knowledge while marking volatile facts for future live checks.

chapters.title: Hira learns when not to trust memory alone
chapters.summary: The assistant gained source-discipline hints and a memory review endpoint.
details: Volatile questions now get an internal live-source requirement before the model answers.
Recommended tools are attached for sports, F1, Liverpool, weather, prayer, news, and pasted links.
Admin memory review exposes bucket counts, recent entries, and pruning rules at /api/admin/memory.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is now less likely to treat stale memory as truth, and its growing memory can be inspected before it gets messy.

chapters.title: Security hardened. Learning loop closed.
chapters.summary: A full code audit closed two critical security gaps and activated four intelligence upgrades that were structurally broken by design.
details: Telegram bot now enforces a user whitelist via HIRA_ALLOWED_USER_IDS — unauthorized users are rejected silently.
Web API now fails closed: if HIRA_WEB_TOKEN is not set, all endpoints return 503 instead of being publicly accessible.
/forget all now requires a second confirmation within 60 seconds to prevent accidental memory wipes.
Memory priority weighting fixed: correction_ledger now shows up to 20 items in the system prompt (was 8), constraints show all, low-signal buckets trimmed.
Weekly memory consolidation job added (Friday 22:30 SGT): Claude reviews corrections and reflections, promotes durable rules into preferences/constraints, and logs a growth note.
Conversation summarisation added: each session is summarised into recent_summaries on /clear or when the history window rolls over — giving H.I.R.A cross-session continuity.
Implicit correction detection added: after each reply, the router model checks if the user is contradicting a specific fact from the previous response, even without explicit correction keywords.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A's learning loop is now fully closed: corrections are captured implicitly and explicitly, consolidated weekly into durable rules, and injected back into context with appropriate priority. The bot can no longer be accessed by strangers.

chapters.title: Security boundaries fail closed by default
chapters.summary: A follow-up review closed the remaining soft edges around Telegram access, web-token guessing, rate-limit identity, notification rendering, and public diagnostics.
details: Telegram startup now fails closed unless HIRA_ALLOWED_USER_IDS is set, with HIRA_TELEGRAM_OPEN_DEV_MODE reserved for explicit local development.
PWA API requests now reject invalid web tokens in middleware with a dedicated auth-failure limiter and constant-time comparison.
Rate limits no longer trust X-Forwarded-For by default; proxy headers require explicit HIRA_TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS opt-in.
Notification rendering now escapes quote characters and whitelists notification kind classes to close attribute-injection risk.
Public /healthz now exposes only minimal status while detailed runtime diagnostics remain behind /api/admin/status.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A now behaves more like a private operating system: missing security config stops access instead of opening it, abuse attempts are slowed earlier, and diagnostic detail stays behind the token.

chapters.title: Hira starts noticing before being asked
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a first proactive intelligence layer plus an intent lens that retrieves relevant memory before answering.
details: New intent lens infers Herwanto's likely hat and intent, then attaches relevant memory from corrections, constraints, reflections, summaries, topic profiles, projects, and source notes.
PWA and Telegram chat now receive this memory-aware lens before source-discipline hints, improving continuity across conversations.
Quick PWA replies also receive the lens, so lightweight chat can still respect durable context without pretending to use tools.
Proactive intelligence scanner now checks workload, due tasks, marking load, follow-ups, quiet windows, and upcoming packed days.
Deduplicated proactive insights run in the PWA worker and Telegram job queue, with configurable interval and active hours.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A can now connect past lessons to present requests and surface early heads-ups like packed-day task pressure or marking pile collisions without waiting for Herwanto to ask.

chapters.title: Hira stops guessing availability
chapters.summary: A scheduling correction tightened H.I.R.A's handling of appointments and after-school slot recommendations.
details: New find_available_training_slots tool checks timetable lessons and Google Calendar before suggesting after-school slots.
Slot planning now supports duration, time window, purpose, and avoid-keywords such as CCA or football.
Requests like best slots, free slots, after school, not during CCA day, and Sahibba training now force the checked availability tool.
Appointment/date questions such as HDB appointment dates now force calendar context instead of relying on memory or inference.
System rules now explicitly say to copy exact appointment dates from tool results and never shift dates by assumption.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is less likely to suggest a training slot on a CCA day or move an appointment by a day; calendar/timetable truth is now mandatory for availability answers.

currentCapabilities: Installable private PWA with chat, agenda, tasks, Gmail, files, uploads, notifications, and dashboard views.
Telegram-compatible tool brain with calendar, reminders, marking, MTL classlists, Gmail, artifacts, weather, MUIS prayer/khutbah, news, web search, and URL reading.
Structured sports intelligence for Liverpool and F1.
Railway Hobby runtime profile with upload queue, memory guardrails, worker split, Redis production option, and admin status.
Long-term memory buckets, topic profiles for new interests, correction ledger, self-reflection notes, source-backed research notes, and conversation summaries.
Intent lens with relevant-memory retrieval before answers, including quick PWA replies.
Proactive intelligence scanner for workload pinch points, marking pressure, follow-ups, quiet windows, and upcoming packed days.
Checked availability planner for after-school training slots that combines timetable lessons, calendar conflicts, and CCA-day avoidance.
Priority-weighted memory prompt: corrections and constraints always visible; low-signal buckets trimmed.
Closed learning loop: implicit + explicit correction capture → weekly consolidation job → promoted to preferences/constraints.
Security: Telegram fail-closed allowlist, web API fail-closed auth, invalid-token throttling, safer notification rendering, /forget confirmation gate, and file upload size guards.
Configurable model routing for quick replies, structured parsing, agentic work, and deep work.
updateProtocol: Append a new chapter to pwa/hira-growth-data.json for every meaningful H.I.R.A upgrade.
Update summary.commitsTracked when major commits are added.
Adjust capabilityRings only when an upgrade changes a real capability, not for copy/UI polish.
Keep live facts out of permanent story text unless they are stable architecture facts.
